member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppContainerExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for publishing container resources as container apps in Azure.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppContainerExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerApp``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure,Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers.ContainerApp})
member.summary: Publishes the specified container resource as a container app.
member.typeparam: The type of the container resource.
member.param: The container resource builder.
The configuration action for the container app.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppCustomizationAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for customizing an Azure Container App.

member.summary: Represents an annotation for customizing an Azure Container App.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppCustomizationAnnotation.Configure
member.summary: Gets the configuration action for customizing the Azure Container App.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource
member.summary: Represents an Azure Container App Environment resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.#ctor(System.String,System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the Container App Environment.
The callback to configure the Azure infrastructure for this resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.EnableDashboard
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Aspire dashboard should be included in the container app environment. Default is true.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.ContainerAppEnvironmentId
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier of the Container App Environment.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.ContainerAppDomain
member.summary: Gets the default domain associated with the Container App Environment.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.ContainerRegistryName
member.summary: Gets the name of the associated Azure Container Registry.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.ContainerRegistryUrl
member.summary: Gets the URL endpoint of the associated Azure Container Registry.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.ContainerRegistryManagedIdentityId
member.summary: Gets the managed identity ID associated with the Azure Container Registry.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.NameOutputReference
member.summary: Gets the name of the Container App Environment.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource.DefaultContainerRegistry
member.summary: Gets the default container registry for this environment.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppResource
member.summary: Represents an Azure Container App resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the resource in the Aspire application model.
Callback to configure the Azure resources.
The target compute resource that this Azure Container App is being created for.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppResource.TargetResource
member.summary: Gets the target resource that this Azure Container App is being created for.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppJobCustomizationAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for customizing an Azure Container App Job.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppJobCustomizationAnnotation.#ctor(System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure,Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers.ContainerAppJob})
member.summary: Represents an annotation for customizing an Azure Container App Job.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppJobCustomizationAnnotation.Configure
member.summary: Gets the configuration action for customizing the Azure Container App Job.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppsInfrastructure
member.summary: Represents the infrastructure for Azure Container Apps within the Aspire Hosting environment.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppsInfrastructure.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureContainerAppsInfrastructure},Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplicationExecutionContext,Microsoft.Extensions.Options.IOptions{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureProvisioningOptions})
member.summary: Represents the infrastructure for Azure Container Apps within the Aspire Hosting environment.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.BaseContainerAppContext.NormalizedContainerAppName
member.summary: The normalized container app name (lowercase) that will be used consistently throughout the container app creation process for both the resource identifier and endpoint mapping host names.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.Utils.BicepFormattingHelpers
member.summary: Provides helper methods for formatting Bicep expressions with specific encodings.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.Utils.BicepFormattingHelpers.FormatBicepExpression(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Formats a Bicep expression using the specified encoding format.
member.param: The value to format, which must be a or .
The encoding format to apply. Currently, only "uri" is supported.
member.exception: Thrown when the value type is not supported for formatting.
Thrown when the specified format is not supported.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExecutableExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for publishing executable resources (with docker files) as container apps in Azure.

member.summary: Publishes the specified container resource as a container app.
member.typeparam: The type of the container resource.
member.param: The container resource builder.
The configuration action for the container app.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for customizing Azure Container App definitions for projects.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExtensions.AddAzureContainerAppsInfrastructure(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder)
member.summary: Adds the necessary infrastructure for Azure Container Apps to the distributed application builder.
member.param: The distributed application builder.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExtensions.AddAzureContainerAppEnvironment(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a container app environment resource to the distributed application builder.
member.param: The distributed application builder.
The name of the resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExtensions.WithAzdResourceNaming(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource})
member.summary: Configures the container app environment resources to use the same naming conventions as azd.
member.param: The AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource to configure.
member.remarks:
By default, the container app environment resources use a different naming convention than azd.
This method allows for reusing the previously deployed resources if the application was deployed using azd without calling

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppExtensions.WithDashboard(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AppContainers.AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Configures whether the Aspire dashboard should be included in the container app environment.
member.param: The AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource to configure.
Whether to include the Aspire dashboard. Default is true.

member.summary: Configures the container app environment resource to use the specified Log Analytics Workspace.
member.param: The AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource to configure.
The resource builder for the to use.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppProjectExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for publishing project resources as container apps in Azure.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureContainerAppProjectExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerApp``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure,Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers.ContainerApp})
member.summary: Allows configuring the specified project resource as a container app.
member.typeparam: The type of the project resource.
member.param: The project resource builder.
The configuration action for the container app.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerAppExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for customizing Azure Container App resource.
Configures the custom domain for the container app.
member.param: The container app resource to configure for custom domain usage.
A resource builder for a parameter resource capturing the name of the custom domain.
A resource builder for a parameter resource capturing the name of the certificate configured in the Azure Portal.
member.exception: Throws if the container app resource is not parented to a .

remarks.para: The extension method simplifies the process of assigning a custom domain to a container app resource when it is deployed. It has no impact on local development.
The method is used in conjunction with the callback. Assigning a custom domain to a container app resource is a multi-step process and requires multiple deployments.

The method takes two arguments which are parameter resource builders. The first is a parameter that represents the custom domain and the second is a parameter that represents the name of the managed certificate provisioned via the Azure Portal

When deploying with custom domains configured for the first time leave the parameter empty (when prompted by the Azure Developer CLI). Once the application is deployed successfully access to the Azure Portal to bind the custom domain to a managed SSL certificate. Once the certificate is successfully provisioned, subsequent deployments of the application can use this certificate name when the is prompted.

For deployments triggered locally by the Azure Developer CLI the config.json file in the .azure/{environment name} path can by modified with the certificate name since Azure Developer CLI will not prompt again for the value.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerAppExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerAppJob``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure,Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers.ContainerAppJob})
member.summary: Allows configuring the specified compute resource as an Azure Container App Job.
member.typeparam: The type of the compute resource.
member.param: The compute resource builder.
The configuration action for the container app job.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerAppExtensions.PublishAsAzureContainerAppJob``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0})
member.summary: Configures the specified compute resource as a manually triggered Azure Container App Job.
member.typeparam: The type of the compute resource.
member.param: The compute resource builder.
member.remarks:
This is a convenience overload for the common case of manually triggered jobs. Manual trigger is the default trigger type, so this method provides a simpler API when no additional job configuration is needed.
builder.AddProject<Projects.ProcessorJob>("processor-job") .PublishAsAzureContainerAppJob(); // Manual trigger (default)

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerAppExtensions.PublishAsScheduledAzureContainerAppJob``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure,Azure.Provisioning.AppContainers.ContainerAppJob})
member.summary: Configures the specified compute resource as a scheduled Azure Container App Job with the provided cron expression.
member.typeparam: The type of the compute resource.
member.param: The compute resource builder.
The cron expression that defines the schedule for the job.
The configuration action for the container app job.
member.remarks:
This method is a convenience wrapper around that automatically configures the job with a schedule trigger using the specified cron expression.
builder.AddProject<Projects.ProcessorJob>("job") .PublishAsScheduledAzureContainerAppJob("0 0 * * *"); // Run every day at midnight
